progressions.name: Ciclo matriarcal (i → iv → V → i)
progressions.summary: The book's primary Eólico cadence. Frigio works as the dominant of Eólico (line 1424); the cycle resolves back to ⋮ (line 1045 'función cíclica').

keys.hint: Resolution / horizon. Solo with the natural minor.
Subdominant. Modal hop — the cross opens motion.
Dominant of Eólico — flamenco color, semitone tension.

progressions.name: Cadencia mayor (I → V7 → I)
progressions.summary: Mixolidio is the dominant 7 of Jónico (line 1439): the major-key authentic cadence in fractal vocabulary. □ ↓ □.

keys.hint: Verticality / major. Solo with the major scale.
Resolution. The book calls Mixolidio 'el punto antes del cierre del mundo hepta mayor'.

progressions.name: Sensible (△ → □)
progressions.summary: Locrio is the leading-tone of Jónico (line 1393): 'siempre aparecerán juntos en la tabla de sonidos (triángulo – cuadrado)'.

keys.hint: Resolution. Triángulo always points at Casita.

progressions.name: 12-station path (DodecaFuga)
progressions.summary: The spine of El Lujo de la Disonancia: walk all 12 stations in the canonical fractal order (D:29315, reused at D:31166 in the DodecaFuga, D:33778, D:37177). One bar per station — every glyph in the system, no repetition, no skipped color.

keys.hint: Casa de Gátople — the home position.

progressions.name: Auro-Boros (cycle of fourths)
progressions.summary: The closed-loop traverse of book 1 (F:1034-1048): start at ⋮, step a perfect fourth (+5 semitones) every move, return to ⋮ with zero net alterations. Pattorres's Cero Pitágoras gesture (F:859-895) extended into a 12-station round trip.

keys.hint: E → A. The serpent eats its tail.
E → A. The serpent eats its tail.

progressions.name: Flamenco / Árabe-Español (+ → ↑)
progressions.summary: The semitone leap from Frigio to Lidio that gives Spanish/Arab music its characteristic color (F:1424). Pattorres specifically names Frigio as the base of flamenco. Frigio dominates Eólico, then resolves home.

keys.hint: Open with the flamenco color — semitone start to Lidio.
Lift +1 semitone — the exotic Árabe-Español sound.
Return — Frigio is dominant of Eólico.

progressions.name: Lidio→Jónico (↑ → □)
progressions.summary: Pattorres's non-standard reading of the major cadence (F:1439): Lidio works as the dominant lead-in to Jónico. Different from the Mixolidio→Jónico cadence — uses the +4 (raised fourth) tension instead of the b7. Worth comparing both in the same key.

keys.hint: Lift — Lidio's #4 is the upper-leading tone.
Compare with Mixolidio→Jónico in the major-cadence preset.

progressions.name: Espejo dodecafráctico (tritone walk)
progressions.summary: The four tritone-axis pairs (D:21259): every glyph has a mirror 6 semitones away. Walk through the four pairs to hear the symmetry — each move is a maximum-distance jump on the wheel (180° opposite).

keys.hint: C# is G's mirror — same axis, opposite pole.
F# is C's mirror — casa de Gátople.
E is B's mirror — close the cycle.

progressions.name: Improvisación pentatónica
progressions.summary: The pentatonic 'no-error' scale (book line 217). All five Penta modes — solo over each in turn. The book argues these are ontologically prior to the heptatonic cadences above.

keys.hint: ★III — Casa de Gátople, the home position.
